findings.title: CreateEventDto.description has no @MaxLength — unbounded DB write
findings.description: The `description` field has `@IsString()` and `@IsOptional()` but no `@MaxLength`. The global ValidationPipe(whitelist:true, forbidNonWhitelisted:true) does not bound the value length. A caller can POST an arbitrarily large string (limited only by network/body size) which gets inserted into PostgreSQL. The Event.description column in schema.prisma is typed as `String?` with no length constraint. The title field correctly has @MaxLength(200).
findings.root_cause: Missing @MaxLength decorator on the optional description field in CreateEventDto.
findings.impact: An authenticated user with events:create permission can insert very large strings into the events table, potentially consuming disk space or causing downstream rendering issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `@MaxLength(5000)` (or an appropriate project-wide limit) to the description field, matching any similar DTO convention in the codebase.
acceptance_criteria: POST /events with description longer than the configured max returns HTTP 400
POST /events with description within the limit succeeds
findings.notes: UpdateEventDto extends PartialType(CreateEventDto) so it inherits the same gap.

findings.title: ICS import processes unbounded VEVENT count with sequential DB writes and no per-field length cap
findings.description: The `importIcs` function iterates over all VEVENTs in a parsed ICS file without any count cap. ImportIcsDto allows up to 5 MB of ICS content, which can contain thousands of VEVENT entries. Each event triggers a sequential `prisma.event.create` call. Additionally, the title (line 253) and description (line 254) extracted from VEVENT fields are truncated by `stripHtml` for HTML tags but are not length-capped before being written to the DB. The global throttle (30 req/s, 600 req/min) limits repeated calls but a single 5MB ICS can still cause a long-running sequential DB write loop.
findings.root_cause: No maximum VEVENT count guard and no per-field @MaxLength truncation on ICS-extracted data before DB insert.
findings.impact: A user with leaves:create permission can upload a large ICS file to trigger a long-running series of DB inserts, degrading API responsiveness. Also allows arbitrarily large title/description strings to be inserted from ICS (bypassing CreateEventDto validators since this path does not go through the DTO).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a MAX_VEVENT_IMPORT constant (e.g. 500) and break early once exceeded. Truncate title to 200 chars and description to 5000 chars after stripHtml. Consider using createMany in a single transaction instead of sequential creates.
acceptance_criteria: POST /planning-export/ics/import with ICS containing >500 VEVENTs returns HTTP 422 or imports only the first 500
ICS VEVENT titles longer than 200 chars are truncated on import
findings.notes: previewImport (line 172-209) has the same no-count-cap issue but is read-only so impact is lower.

findings.title: Unvalidated date query strings passed to new Date() — possible HTTP 500 instead of 400
findings.description: The `start` and `end` query parameters in GET /planning-export/ics and `startDate`/`endDate` in GET /events/range are accepted as raw strings from the controller with no @IsISO8601 or @IsDateString validation. They are passed directly to `new Date()`. If the string is not a valid date (e.g. 'foobar'), `new Date('foobar')` produces `Invalid Date`, and passing NaN to Prisma's date filter will likely cause a runtime error (HTTP 500) rather than a clean HTTP 400 Bad Request. The PlanningOverviewQueryDto (planning module) correctly uses @IsISO8601, demonstrating the right pattern.
findings.root_cause: No DTO with @IsDateString/@IsISO8601 validation wrapping the date query parameters in exportIcs and getEventsByRange.
findings.impact: An authenticated user can trigger HTTP 500 responses by sending malformed date strings, leaking internal error information and impacting observability signal quality.
findings.suggested_fix: Create a typed query DTO for GET /planning-export/ics and GET /events/range using @IsISO8601 + @IsOptional on the date parameters, matching the PlanningOverviewQueryDto pattern.
acceptance_criteria: GET /planning-export/ics?start=notadate returns HTTP 400 with a validation error message
findings.notes: events.service.ts:getEventsByRange lines 660-665 has isNaN checks that catch the Invalid Date before DB write — so the 500 path is more likely in exportIcs which has no such guard.

findings.title: getEventsByRange and findOne RBAC filter defaults to unscoped on null role — inconsistent with findAll
findings.description: In `getEventsByRange` (lines 681-690) and `findOne` (line 390-403), the RBAC scope filter is applied only when `currentUserId && currentUserRole` are both truthy. If `currentUserRole` is null, the filter block is skipped and the query is unscoped (returns all events). By contrast, `findAll` (lines 277-290) calls `getPermissionsForRole(null)` which correctly returns `[]`, and applies the scope filter because `!permissions.includes('events:readAll')` is true. Today, PermissionsGuardV2 (global APP_GUARD, enforce mode) rejects all null-role callers before reaching the controller, so this code path is unreachable in normal operation. However, the default-open pattern is fragile: if the guard configuration changes, or if these service methods are called internally with null roles, the scope filter would silently be omitted.
findings.root_cause: Defensive RBAC filter uses `if (currentUserId && currentUserRole)` (default-open) instead of unconditionally calling `getPermissionsForRole` (which safely returns [] for null role), inconsistent with the findAll pattern.
findings.impact: Not currently exploitable (blocked by PermissionsGuardV2). If guard configuration changes or internal callers pass null roles, would expose all events without scope filter.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `if (currentUserId && currentUserRole)` with unconditional `getPermissionsForRole(currentUserRole)` call (handles null safely), matching the findAll pattern.
acceptance_criteria: getEventsByRange applies scope filter even when currentUserRole is null
findOne applies IDOR check even when currentUserRole is null
findings.notes: findOne IDOR check: line 390: `if (currentUserId && currentUserRole && !(await this.hasManagementAccess(currentUserRole)))` — same default-open pattern.

verified_clean: apps/api/src/events/events.controller.ts — all routes carry @RequirePermissions or @AllowSelfService; mutation routes use @UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, OwnershipGuard) + @OwnershipCheck; getEventsByUser has inline permission check for cross-user access
apps/api/src/events/events.service.ts — no raw SQL / $queryRawUnsafe; all DB queries use Prisma parameterized queries; ensureCanMutate provides service-layer defense-in-depth ownership check

apps/api/src/events/dto/create-event.dto.ts — title has @MaxLength(200) @MinLength(3); time fields have @Matches regex; IDs are @IsUUID; global ValidationPipe(whitelist:true, forbidNonWhitelisted:true) prevents mass-assignment
apps/api/src/planning/planning.controller.ts — single GET route protected by @RequirePermissions('users:read'); @CurrentUser used for scoping; no sensitive data hardcoded
apps/api/src/planning/planning.service.ts — aggregates sub-service calls each of which applies its own RBAC; no raw SQL; no hardcoded secrets

apps/api/src/planning-export/planning-export.controller.ts — exportIcs protected by @AllowSelfService (JWT still required, user scoped to own data); import routes protected by @RequirePermissions('leaves:create'); user-agent capped at 512 chars
apps/api/src/planning-export/dto/import-ics.dto.ts — @MaxLength(5*1024*1024) prevents oversized ICS payloads at DTO layer

apps/api/src/planning-export/planning-export.service.ts — stripHtml() applied to all ICS-extracted free-text fields (not susceptible to ReDoS — negated char class); Prisma parameterized queries throughout; audit trail recorded for data export (OBS-007); no hardcoded secrets
apps/api/src/events/events.service.ts:findAll — null-role path is correctly default-closed via getPermissionsForRole(null) returning []
apps/api/src/planning-export/planning-export.service.ts:exportIcs — self-service scoped: queries always filtered by userId from JWT; no cross-user data leakage
